title: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1
display_title: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1
wikitext:
[]: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by xnatsu23 » Sat Jan 25, 2014 9:34 am Well the most popular theory it that when Rei 3 become god, she can transcend time and space however I don't agree with that because she does not have a reason to appear to Shinji. So this is my theory, "what if the start of eva(ep 1) isn't the start of eva" instead it begins in episode 25 and 26, the "human instrumentality project". We all know that Shinji has the choice whether to reject the instrumentality or not. Rei gives this right to Shinji, and while he is trapped in the instrumentality Rei is guiding Shinji to come up with his own answer, so I think that ep 1- 24 are just memories of Shinji being told in the instrumentality project before he come up with his own answer and reject the instrumentality. the Rei from the start is just a signal that the story is starting and the Rei from EOE signals the end of story(also the instrumentality project).. so does it make any sense??

[]: Re: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Sat Jan 25, 2014 10:01 am xnatsu23 wrote: so does it make any sense??

[]: Re: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by xnatsu23 » Sat Jan 25, 2014 1:58 pm Gorbatschow wrote: Okay, Let's assume this: - What if episode 1 isn't the start of Eva (chronologically) but rather the end. What if Shinji was killed by Sachiel and he made everything that is going to happen up, including the events of Ep1 (in order to not contradict his own "reality") and the Rei that appeared was only part of it ? This would be supported by "near death experiences" and some "neuro-science" I heard about ... I guess. - What If everything is just a day dream Shinji has while being in class ? I hope you see what I'm trying to say: You can't prove such theories wrong, despite or rather because there is no evidence to prove them right. Therefore I must ask myself: Why should I believe your theory, when there's nearly no reason to assume it's correct ? It makes sense and fills (in my opinion not existing) gaps, but there isn't a single clue to begin with (Kind of like the idea behind every religion*cough* *cough*) In order to show you what I mean, please answer the following questions: - Why don't you believe that Rei became a god ? - If she isn't a god, how could she save Misato, Ritsuko, etc. ? (she traveled back in time, remember ?) - What's your theory's foundation ? Last but not least a thread addressing among other facets of Rei's appearance your question. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jan 25, 2014 2:36 pm Time travel is only a "power" if you construct your model of how it works from a particular perspective. Time travel is not only possible, you're doing it right now. You're traveling from the past to the future at a rate of sixty seconds per minute. Lilith's "time travel" is not time travel at all. Lilith perceives reality in a way that you can't. Let's imagine for a moment that there is a two dimensional creature. It lives in a two dimensional space. It can't look "up", it can only perceive two directions. Now, you, being a three dimensional creature, look down on this 2D being on its infinitely wide and long but infinitely thin plane, and see it moving it around. You take your hands and put one finger down at two distinct points on the plane. To the two dimensional being, there's a finger here and a finger there, but from your perspective you are still a singular entity, touching two points simultaneously. Now we go up a couple of dimensions. We think of ourselves as 3d creatures but we're really folding the bottom three dimensions through the fourth, so to speak. We can spread out through three dimensions but we're limited to a single point in the fourth, which is time. Lilith is (at least) one dimension higher than that. She is to us as we are to the two-dimensional creature walking around on the flat plane. When you look at another person, you see them at one point in the first four dimensions. Lilith would see a person as a cloud or snake, able to perceive every position they've ever occupied or will occupy at once. Just like you can reach down and confuse the two dimensional creature by appearing in two places at once, Rei can touch two points on Shinji's timeline at once (or more). To the mere mortal she appears to be "traveling" but from her perspective she hasn't gone anywhere. Lilith can appear as a ghostly Rei to Shinji in the first episode because she's not longer bound by linear time, just as you're not bound by the 2D plane in the analogy. From the proper perspective, "time travel" in this form is no more a type of travel than your possessing a right and left hand is a form of travel. It's all about point of view. Depending on how far above us her perspective is raised she may well perceive individuals not only as a cloud of all places they are, will be, and have been, but as all places they could ever be . The angels don't demonstrate the ability to do this (or rather, be this) because they're not the Lilith-Adam-Rei-Kaworu entity. They're two rungs down the ladder, so to speak. Time is just another aspect of space.

[]: Re: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jan 25, 2014 3:19 pm xnatsu23 wrote: Well the most popular theory it that when Rei 3 become god, she can transcend time and space however I don't agree with that because she does not have a reason to appear to Shinji.

[]: Re: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by xnatsu23 » Sat Jan 25, 2014 6:26 pm yes, you are right I am trying to achieve linearity by using the concurrency theory while trying to explain this Rei part mystery. Now that I think about the fact that Shinji recognized Rei directly negates my theory, however still your theory about the omnipotent hybrid is still too far-fetched to me, although it make sense. She is a caring, nice person, it's not her fault that she was raised to be a soldier and never really taught to interact with people/ that she has value/relevance beyond "fulfilling her purpose". That's part of makes her tragic. Well, "the hybrid got transcendent" does, as it does with the bookend Reis themselves. And it even ties them to previously shown/established phenomena like the Reis who spawned during impact,

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Sat Jan 25, 2014 6:45 pm What you're trying to say is, that you don't see an intention for Rei's action because you assume she doesn't possess the memories of Shinji and, based on this, you make up a theory which completely lacks any prove, ignores more or less obvious facts and kind of screws with many assumption that can be considered facts ? 1. We see that Ritsukos / Misatos body disappeared after Rei appeared in front of her. 2. How exactly does it matter that Rei affected the present ? 3. We clearly see through the whole show, that Rei's death don't go along with loss of memories. 4. Rei possesses Lilith's soul and (after she betrayed Gendo and absorbed Adam) Lilith's body. If Lilith would be able to control Rei, she would be controlled by her trough the whole show. (Which wouldn't change a thing) 5. I don't see how the concept of dimensions is "too complicated" since it applies to us every day. Time itself is "4D", we are only able to perceive three dimension completely and the fourth partially. Same is with a two-dimensional creature. It can perceive 2 dimension completely and the third partially (You can imagine it like CTs (endless 2 dimensional pictures)). Traveling through time is nothing more than operating four-dimensional. And Rei/Lilith/God is obviously able to do so. 6. Assuming you approve the "Rei remembers stuff after her death"-theory (I would call it an observation), Kendrix showed Rei's intentions for Betraying Gendo / Appearing in front of Shinji / Letting Shinji decide.

[]: Re: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by Sephizim » Sat Jan 25, 2014 7:05 pm xnatsu23 wrote: I totally agree that Rei is indeed a nice and caring person, however you should note that this Rei died. Since it seems that the Rei's memories are personal, Rei 3's memories should be different to the Rei 2, however since they share the same eva, It is possible that there are memory leaks while in synchronized with the eva, however the memory being leaked are her intense memories like with Shinji. Which would mean that hypothetically her memories with dr akagi and Misato will be minimal, so then why would this hybrid Rei saved them if she could possibly have not known them in personal level.

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Sat Jan 25, 2014 7:51 pm In order to enjoy any form of media, you have to realize that the opinions of the creators may vary with your own. Just because you view time as working in a certain way, the creators of this show have taken a definite stance on how time works in the context of the show. If that's what you believe in real life, that's fine, but you're going to have to go through a lot of mental gymnastics to make the show's logic match your real life logic. That's why its fiction. If you can accept Freudian robot moms that shoot religious symbolism, you should be able to believe non-linear time paradoxes.

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Sat Jan 25, 2014 7:53 pm xnatsu23 wrote: 1 Yes i know, so which Rei is it? the hybrid or the Rei accompanied with the anti-at field. 2 well I just do not agree that the hybrid Rei should interfere with the present. 3 not really, Rei 1's memories are different to Rei 2, also even if the memories continues, it doesn't mean that their personal feelings continues, the third Rei has different perspective than Rei 2 4 ok, you're right then, Rei 3 has the control then. 5 It is just that I don't agree with that reasoning and logic because I classify time as something that is finite, It's like this while you are alive you consume your time, this is called living. so time past can never be recovered. So when an omnipotent being from the future alter or affect your "present", that doesn't make sense to me. which is what the hybrid Rei do, she should have stayed in her domain. 6 no I don't agree with that.

[]: Re: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by xnatsu23 » Sat Jan 25, 2014 8:01 pm I'm assuming this is in reference to the fact that when Rei III first appears in episode 23, she seems to not remember saving Shinji. However, if you observe literally everything else she says and does, it seems to be a continuation of the memories of Rei II. In the hospital, her distance is most likely due to her trying to reconcile memories that are present in her mind and soul, but that she never actually went through herself. She has the memories, but not the experience of having done those things, which is probably very hard for her to sort out. This is also the reason for her "I am seeing these for the first time and yet not the first time" line when looking at Gendo's glasses. Also, considering the widely accepted theory that after Rei I's death, part of her soul was been divided up and placed into Unit-00, and then after Unit-00's destruction, her whole soul was placed into Rei III, she would be justifiably unsure of everything.

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Sat Jan 25, 2014 8:09 pm Gorbatschow wrote: How would you explain back to the future, xnatsu ?

[]: Re: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Sat Jan 25, 2014 8:10 pm ^ I kinda messed the quotation up. I was referring to the whole "not-acknowledging-the-rules"-topic. ====== xnatsu23 wrote: so if the hybrid Rei was used as a mark, which memories are used in Shinji so that she could be driven into a corner? where did the Misato and Asuka scenes came from? Because hypothetically their still on their body and Rei shouldn't have access to them yet.

[]: Re: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by xnatsu23 » Sat Jan 25, 2014 8:12 pm Gorbatschow wrote: Why shouldn't she have access ? Mistato, Ritsuko and Asuka were already collected when Instumentality started. The Anti-AT-Field had already collected their souls, when Shinji dived into the HIP-scenes. Memories used in Shinji ?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jan 25, 2014 8:13 pm xnatsu23 wrote: too complicated for me but I get your point, so then what is her reason appearing to Shinji? If it is really a problem of just Shinji's perspective then I can assume that the Rei appeared to Shinji should do doing the same thing from another place right? then how would you explain that at that current time lilith is still being imprison in nerv. Also how can a 3d entity manages to kill a 4d entity.

[]: Re: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Sat Jan 25, 2014 8:14 pm xnatsu23 wrote: UM pre-instumentality, it is before the massive anti-at field. before where do you think the instrumentality started??

[]: Re: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by Sephizim » Sat Jan 25, 2014 8:14 pm xnatsu23 wrote: so if the hybrid Rei was used as a mark, which memories are used in Shinji so that she could be driven into a corner? where did the Misato and Asuka scenes came from? Because hypothetically their still on their body and Rei shouldn't have access to them yet.

[]: Re: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Sat Jan 25, 2014 8:21 pm Sephizim wrote: I'm a little confused about what you mean about being "driven into a corner." But regarding Pre-3I Instrumentality, that is still a topic the general community can't really seem to agree on. It does seem like Shinji, Misato, Rei, Asuka and Ritsuko are all having a dialog before the collection of souls even starts. As for an explanation for that goes, I think one of the forum veterans would probably be better qualified than me to expound on that.

Time travel is not only possible, you're doing it right now. You're traveling from the past to the future at a rate of sixty seconds per minute. Lilith's "time travel" is not time travel at all. Lilith perceives reality in a way that you can't. Let's imagine for a moment that there is a two dimensional creature. It lives in a two dimensional space. It can't look "up", it can only perceive two directions. Now, you, being a three dimensional creature, look down on this 2D being on its infinitely wide and long but infinitely thin plane, and see it moving it around. You take your hands and put one finger down at two distinct points on the plane. To the two dimensional being, there's a finger here and a finger there, but from your perspective you are still a singular entity, touching two points simultaneously. Now we go up a couple of dimensions. We think of ourselves as 3d creatures but we're really folding the bottom three dimensions through the fourth, so to speak. Article: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1
 ? 1. We see that Ritsukos / Misatos body disappeared after Rei appeared in front of her. 2. How exactly does it matter that Rei affected the present ? 3. We clearly see through the whole show, that Rei's death don't go along with loss of memories. 4. Rei possesses Lilith's soul and (after she betrayed Gendo and absorbed Adam) Lilith's body. If Lilith would be able to control Rei, she would be controlled by her trough the whole show. (Which wouldn't change a thing) 5. I don't see how the concept of dimensions is "too complicated" since it applies to us every day. Time itself is "4D", we are only able to perceive three dimension completely and the fourth partially. Same is with a two-dimensional creature. It can perceive 2 dimension completely and the third partially (You can imagine it like CTs (endless 2 dimensional pictures)). Traveling through time is nothing more than operating four-dimensional. And Rei/Lilith/God is obviously able to do so. 6. Assuming you approve the "Rei remembers stuff after her death"-theory (I would call it an observation), Kendrix showed Rei's intentions for Betraying Gendo / Appearing in front of Shinji / Letting Shinji decide.

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Sat Jan 25, 2014 7:51 pm In order to enjoy any form of media, you have to realize that the opinions of the creators may vary with your own. Just because you view time as working in a certain way, the creators of this show have taken a definite stance on how time works in the context of the show. If that's what you believe in real life, that's fine, but you're going to have to go through a lot of mental gymnastics to make the show's logic match your real life logic. That's why its fiction. If you can accept Freudian robot moms that shoot religious symbolism, you should be able to believe non-linear time paradoxes.

[]: Re: Why did Rei III appear in Episode 1?             Quote Post by xnatsu23 » Sat Jan 25, 2014 8:01 pm I'm assuming this is in reference to the fact that when Rei III first appears in episode 23, she seems to not remember saving Shinji. However, if you observe literally everything else she says and does, it seems to be a continuation of the memories of Rei II. In the hospital, her distance is most likely due to her trying to reconcile memories that are present in her mind and soul, but that she never actually went through herself. She has the memories, but not the experience of having done those things, which is probably very hard for her to sort out. This is also the reason for her "I am seeing these for the first time and yet not the first time" line when looking at Gendo's glasses. Also, considering the widely accepted theory that after Rei I's death, part of her soul was been divided up and placed into Unit-00, and then after Unit-00's destruction, her whole soul was placed into Rei III, she would be justifiably unsure of everything.
